observed.excerpt: step-loop.ts:187: `agentSpec = compileAgentSpec(agentSpec, resolvedInput, options.templateEnv);` — still 3 args, no cwd. Sibling step-agent.ts:133: `agentSpec = compileAgentSpec(agentSpec, resolvedInput, templateEnv, ctx.cwd);` — 4 args. The asymmetry the finding describes is unchanged at HEAD; loop-dispatched agents still compile without cwd, so contextBlocks injection is silently skipped for them. Correction: finding paths omit the `packages/` prefix (repo layout is packages/<pkg>/); line numbers match exactly.

agent-spec.ts:31: `if (value.endsWith(".md") || value.endsWith(".txt") || (value.includes("/") && !value.includes("\n"))) {` — the `/`-in-single-line branch is verbatim unchanged; a single-line inline prompt containing `/` is still classified as a template path. Correction: finding cites line 25; current line is 31 (function opens at 26) — count divergence only, substance stands.

parseModelSpec (index.ts:1176) unchanged: `const slashIndex = spec.indexOf("/"); if (slashIndex !== -1) { return { provider: spec.slice(0, slashIndex), modelId: spec.slice(slashIndex + 1) }; } return { provider: "anthropic", modelId: spec };` — the anthropic default for slash-less ids is verbatim at :1181, exactly the cited line. Still called from the classify path (:1396).

ABSENCE: no compile-time format-vs-tools conflict check exists. Greps for format+tool/conflict across agent-spec.ts and compile.ts return no validation — tools pass through untested (`tools: spec.tools` at agent-spec.ts:253 and compile.ts:590; `outputFormat: spec.output?.format` at agent-spec.ts:261, no cross-check). Mooting status re-verified: jit-runtime.ts:2-5 still states 'In-process LLM dispatch with phantom-tool structured output enforcement… no tools (only the phantom output tool)', and .context FGAP-124 (closed) records 'In-process jit dispatch cannot execute tools — executeAgent is a single-turn completion primitive'. So the partial-moot stands (declared tools never execute in-process), but the declared defect — no conflict check at compile time — is unchanged.
observed.method: grep -n 'tools' src/agent-spec.ts src/compile.ts; grep -n 'conflict|format' src/agent-spec.ts src/compile.ts src/types.ts; grep -n 'phantom' src/jit-runtime.ts; python content search of .context/framework-gaps.json for FGAP-124

observed.excerpt: RESOLVED by commit 1bc36ba0 'feat(pi-jit-agents,pi-behavior-monitors): implement issue-023 monitor classify trace capture (canonical-pi-aligned)'. index.ts:1299 `resolveTraceSettings(monitorName)` resolves `--trace`/`--no-trace`/`--trace-dir`/`--trace-filter` CLI flags plus per-monitor trace-config.json; the classify call wires it at :1410 (`const { tracePath, redactionConfigPath } = resolveTraceSettings(monitor.name);`) and passes tracePath into dispatch (:1450). trace-writer.ts is a schema-validated, lockfile-atomic JSONL writer ('accepts an already-redacted TraceEntry, validates it against packages/pi-jit-agents/schemas/agent-trace.schema.json, and atomically appends'). agent-trace.schema.json defines the variant chain 'session_start (root) → classify_call → { classify_response, context_collection } → verdict_decision; trace_end'. Monitor classify misfires are now inspectable — the exact capability issue-023 asked for.
observed.method: grep -n 'traceClassify|PI_MONITOR|DEBUG|trace' packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts; grep -n 'resolveTraceSettings|tracePath' index.ts; sed -n 1,60p packages/pi-jit-agents/src/trace-writer.ts; git log --oneline --diff-filter=A -- packages/pi-jit-agents/src/trace-writer.ts; git log -S resolveTraceSettings -- packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts

observed.excerpt: PARTIAL.
